body:

------

[Continue to review full report at Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/coder/coder/pull/20?src=pr&el=continue&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=coder).
> **Legend** - [Click here to learn more](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/codecov-delta?utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=coder)
> `Δ = absolute <relative> (impact)`, `ø = not affected`, `? = missing data`
> Powered by [Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/coder/coder/pull/20?src=pr&el=footer&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=coder). Last update [92710ed...f61d9ea](https://codecov.io/gh/coder/coder/pull/20?src=pr&el=lastupdated&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=coder). Read the [comment docs](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/pull-request-comments?utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=coder).

The error in the PR here is really strange:
```
WARNING: DATA RACE
Read at 0x00c0003c44f0 by goroutine 123:
  github.com/pion/ice/v2.(*Agent).Close()
      /home/runner/go/pkg/mod/github.com/pion/ice/v2@v2.1.18/agent.go:899 +0xde
  github.com/pion/webrtc/v3.(*ICEGatherer).Close()
      /home/runner/go/pkg/mod/github.com/pion/webrtc/v3@v3.1.13/icegatherer.go:184 +0xd9
  github.com/pion/webrtc/v3.(*ICETransport).Stop()
      /home/runner/go/pkg/mod/github.com/pion/webrtc/v3@v3.1.13/icetransport.go:197 +0x176
  github.com/pion/webrtc/v3.(*PeerConnection).Close()
      /home/runner/go/pkg/mod/github.com/pion/webrtc/v3@v3.1.13/peerconnection.go:1905 +0x724
  github.com/coder/coder/peer.(*Conn).closeWithError()
      /home/runner/work/coder/coder/peer/conn.go:494 +0x414
  github.com/coder/coder/peer.(*Conn).CloseWithError()
      /home/runner/work/coder/coder/peer/conn.go:460 +0x51
  github.com/coder/coder/peer_test.TestConn.func9()
      /home/runner/work/coder/coder/peer/conn_test.go:201 +0xb6
  testing.tRunner()
      /opt/hostedtoolcache/go/1.17.6/x64/src/testing/testing.go:1259 +0x22f
  testing.(*T).Run·dwrap·21()
      /opt/hostedtoolcache/go/1.17.6/x64/src/testing/testing.go:1306 +0x47
```

Haven't seen it before in any of our runs. I wonder if the `--count` option is doing something undesirable, like running individual tests in parallel?

An alternative is we could consider having a helper script that runs the `gotestsum` command multiple times, like [from this stackoverflow](https://serverfault.com/questions/273238/how-to-run-a-command-multiple-times-using-bash-shell):
```
run() {
    number=$1
    shift
    for i in `seq $number`; do
      $@
    done
}
```

The count is actually from the Go test CLI. It essentially runs the command multiple times.

Unfortunately, we'll have to use my fork of `pion/ice`, until my PR gets merged.
